title: pternandra affinis
intro: Pternandra affinis, also known as the Malay apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving digestive issues. Pternandra affinis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in folk medicine in Southeast Asia, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
